Accountability coach rolls. So how do you become an amazing accountability coach? As a quick reminder, He Who or She Who reaches out the most to their front-end customers will retain the most customers in the end. So accountability is everything. It is the main differentiator for this program, for why we can sell, like why we can sell memberships that are so much more expensive than everyone else, why we can occupy lifetime in Equinox, and all these big gyms that have group training is because of the accountability. So do not skimp on this, be proactive and understand that this is the major value that you are providing people and this is why people are going to stay and pay. Alright, so we have a little sheet here. It's very straightforward, but essentially you want to be able to have pre-canned responses for the different scenarios that might happen for a client, so especially your trainer, so whoever is managing the accountability, they need to understand this piece. And hopefully if they actually want to help people, understanding that accountability is the thing that helps people not getting someone to get their sweat on, but actually making them believe that they can finish and actually coaching someone, being a coach and helping them through the hard times and helping get them back on the horse when they don't show up two or three days in a row, that is what people are paying for. That is actually what happens when you need to change someone's life, not coach someone on a squat better. Alright, I want to be really clear there. Like if you actually want to help people, this is how you do it. Alright, so most important piece, each week block out two hours, you can use Vox or you can just message whatever way you want to message people you can, but message all clients about the results you can use front app and Twilio and just text people from there, but each week you want to block out two hours to do that. Alright, just to make sure that you're reaching out to everyone at least once a week who is in the front end programs. As they transition to membership, so you can decrease that cadence to once every other week to continue to retain them. Just FYI. Okay, scenario one, someone lost good weight, so they lost a lot of weight. Hey, I saw that you lost seven pounds just reaching out to get you a virtual high five, incredibly solid weeks one through whatever it is week one through two. So I see that you're now down for a total of x over x weeks, seven weeks they've been there. Personally, you really crushed it on like the yesterday way to do it Rosie. Love having you and please don't hesitate to reach out if there's anything I can do. Right, really simple. You can have a copy and pasted keep it in your phone notes, and you can text it out. Alright, so very straightforward. Scenario two is they lost a little bit of weight. So really simple, except this one, the last sentence changes like love having you. And but I see like you're a little slower this week, is there anything out of the ordinary that happened anything you're stressed about anything through your off track, right? Just checking it. Right now, someone stays the same. Right. Hey, John, see you didn't have the best week, just checking in shovel shoot. What's going on, right? And did not report weight is, hey, you know, didn't see your numbers this week, just checking in pulse check seeing if you're alive, all that good stuff, like by all means be humorous, make leave emojis and bit emojis, and, you know, make people laugh so that this feels personal. Alright, and this is the point it's coaching is checking in and see how they're doing. Now, correct problems related to non results. The way that we ran through this is there's a three step follow through. First one, if someone says they haven't received results or whatever it's how many days have you doing all three meals and the exact amounts and all the supplements, right? Because they're like, I'm following everything and you're cool how many days a week have you had all three meals in the exact amounts and the right orders and all the supplements they say for any say cool and you do that seven that resolves it. If they then respond to seven or the first move on the second one, how many weeks we give you at all three meals and the exact amounts of them seven, I did everything perfectly. I can't help if you don't tell me the truth, right? And if they're being really truthful, then have them take a picture of their meals and send it to you for the next seven days. It's very rare that this doesn't fix it because a lot of times you'll either discover that they thought that bananas and butter was part of the meal plan or whatever. You know what I mean? Like something crazy. Well, actually they could eat bananas butter, but you know, they thought ice cream was part of the meal plan. You would be amazed, right? Point is if you see if they send you the pictures that will do one of two things. One, you'll find out what they're doing wrong. Second, just by sending the pictures, they'll actually do what they said they were doing and then they'll lose weight. And then in a very, very, very, very rare situation, I'm talking like I've had this happen three times in my life. Someone actually sent me all of their meals and everything looked according to plan. And then at that point, we go in and kind of explain the difference about like water weight gain and how like if you stress out, then sometimes your water, you can continue to hold more water as you're losing fat. And then all of a sudden sometimes two weeks, three weeks in, someone loses like six pounds. It happens. All right. And the way that we kind of forced that is that we switch them to a carb cycling protocol and we cycle their carb mostly so that we can drop any of this additional water weight if we can with that protocol. So that's kind of the intention there. It's like, okay, obviously if you're following all these things, like you don't have a super metabolism. Like as in not like a good metabolism, but like you don't have some frequent metabolism, we just need to figure out how we can stop hiding the real fat loss with the weight of the water that is continually increasing as your fat is going down. And so that is what we do there. That's the carb cycling protocol. So that is the main dealio. Just make sure that you're checking the Facebook group a couple times a day, and then make sure that you dedicate two hours once a week to reach out to everyone and then about two 15 minute time blocks a day, just to check in with the people who are messaging you during the week, which is just good customer service, which you probably do already. All right. That is it. That is the kind of the coach roles. Enjoy.
